headline: US awards $500 million to Nvidia-backed SandboxAQ for finding new chipmaking materials
summary: The U.S. government awarded $500 million on Wednesday to startup SandboxAQ to develop new chemicals and materials for domestic semiconductor manufacturing, including alternatives to PFAS and rare earth imports.  The award is part of President Donald ‌Trump's move to allocate research funds under the CHIPS Act, an effort that previously funded a $150 million investment in new chip manufacturing ‌tools and a $2 billion investment in quantum computing.  So far, SandboxAQ has applied those models to fields such as biotechnology and quantum navigation sensors to replace global positioning systems, but now it is moving into chemicals and materials for chip manufacturing.

content.title: US awards $500 million to Nvidia-backed SandboxAQ for finding new chipmaking materials
content.summary: The U.S. government awarded $500 million on Wednesday to startup SandboxAQ to develop new chemicals and materials for domestic semiconductor manufacturing, including alternatives to PFAS and rare earth imports.  The award is part of President Donald ‌Trump's move to allocate research funds under the CHIPS Act, an effort that previously funded a $150 million investment in new chip manufacturing ‌tools and a $2 billion investment in quantum computing.  So far, SandboxAQ has applied those models to fields such as biotechnology and quantum navigation sensors to replace global positioning systems, but now it is moving into chemicals and materials for chip manufacturing.

headline: Nvidia locks in $25B of high-grade bonds, adding to AI sprint
summary: Nvidia Corp. on June 15 priced $25 billion of investment-grade senior notes in seven parts spanning maturities from 2028 through 2056, as AI and data center expansion activity boosts the company’s credit standing. It marked the first $10 billion-plus deal since April 30, when Meta cleared $25 billion for AI-related liquidity. The only larger high-grade deal this year came from Amazon ($37 billion in March), and it joins other $25 billion placements this year from Oracle (Feb. 2) and Salesforce (

summary: According to a recently published report by Dell'Oro Group, the trusted source for market information about the telecommunications, security, networks, and data center industries, the worldwide revenue for Data Center IT Semiconductors and Components for servers and storage systems increased 116 percent year-over-year in 1Q 2026, propelled by ongoing AI Infrastructure expansion and rising memory prices.

content.title: Data Center IT Semiconductor and Component Revenue Increased 116 Percent in 1Q 2026, According to Dell'Oro Group
content.summary: According to a recently published report by Dell'Oro Group, the trusted source for market information about the telecommunications, security, networks, and data center industries, the worldwide revenue for Data Center IT Semiconductors and Components for servers and storage systems increased 116 percent year-over-year in 1Q 2026, propelled by ongoing AI Infrastructure expansion and rising memory prices.
